citations.narrative:
There are 19 dogs observed in the kennel but the APHIS 7005 lists only 10 dogs. The nine dogs not listed on the APHIS
7005 are juvenile puppies (approximately 6 months of age). The licensee stated that they will be sold soon so did not add
them to the inventory. Records of all the dogs must be made and maintained with accurate information so their identity
can be known with certainty, and to be able to track the dogs that are purchased, transported, traded, sold, or leave by
other methods and for accurate health care documentation.
Each dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly disclose the following information
concerning each dog purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, or otherwise in his or her possession or under his or
her control, or which is transported, euthanized, sold, or otherwise disposed of by that dealer. The records shall include
any offspring born of any animal while in his or her possession or under his or her control. The APHIS 7005 must be
correctly completed and maintained.
To be corrected by 6 March 2026

The contingency plan was last signed 9/24/2022. The licensee stated that nothing has changed so why look at it. An
annual review of the plan allows for making changes based on personnel, equipment and number of dogs. The plan must
be reviewed by the dealer on at least an annual basis to ensure that it adequately addresses the criteria listed in
paragraph (a) of this section. Each licensee and registrant must maintain documentation of their annual reviews. The
licensee must review and document that review of the contingency plan.
To be corrected by 6 March 2026

Inside the building part of the shelter housing facility, there was a newly opened bag of dog food on the floor. Open bags
of food can attract pests and insects that can contaminate the food, decreasing the palatability and nutritional value. All
open supplies of food must be kept in leakproof containers with tightly fitting lids to prevent contamination and spoilage.
The licensee must place any and all open bags of food in a leakproof container with a tightly fitting lid at all times.
To be corrected by 6 March 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.75(a)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
There are 19 dogs observed in the kennel but the APHIS 7005 lists only 10 dogs. The nine dogs not listed on the APHIS 
7005 are juvenile puppies (approximately 6 months of age). The licensee stated that they will be sold soon so did not add 
them to the inventory. Records of all the dogs must be made and maintained with accurate information so their identity 
can be known with certainty, and to be able to track the dogs that are purchased, transported, traded, sold, or leave by 
other methods and for accurate health care documentation.  
Each dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records or forms which fully and correctly disclose the following information 
concerning each dog purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, or otherwise in his or her possession or under his or 
her control, or which is transported, euthanized, sold, or otherwise disposed of by that dealer. The records shall include 
any offspring born of any animal while in his or her possession or under his or her control. The APHIS 7005 must be 
correctly completed and maintained. 
 
To be corrected by 6 March 2026 
2.134(b) 
   
Contingency planning. 
The contingency plan was last signed 9/24/2022. The licensee stated that nothing has changed so why look at it. An 
annual review of the plan allows for making changes based on personnel, equipment and number of dogs. The plan must 
be reviewed by the dealer on at least an annual basis to ensure that it adequately addresses the criteria listed in 
paragraph (a) of this section. Each licensee and registrant must maintain documentation of their annual reviews. The 
licensee must review and document that review of the contingency plan. 
 
To be corrected by 6 March 2026 
3.1(e) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
Inside the building part of the shelter housing facility, there was a newly opened bag of dog food on the floor. Open bags 
of food can attract pests and insects that can contaminate the food, decreasing the palatability and nutritional value. All 
open supplies of food must be kept in leakproof containers with tightly fitting lids to prevent contamination and spoilage.

The licensee must place any and all open bags of food in a leakproof container with a tightly fitting lid at all times. 
 
To be corrected by 6 March 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative. 
 
 n
